compressed_prompt: Compose song lyrics about a socio-economic problem. The song should be in English and in all lowercase letters.
response:
**"bread and circus"**

*(verse 1)*
they built the towers high,
but the floors don’t hold us.
rent’s a knife in the dark,
and the landlord’s always colder.
we clock in, we clock out,
but the numbers don’t add up.
just enough to drown,
but never enough to swim.

*(pre-chorus)*
oh, they say pull yourself up,
like the bootstraps grow on trees.
but the ladder’s made of glass,
and the fall’s a guarantee.

*(chorus)*
this is bread and circus,
feed the machine, keep us hungry.
promise us the moon,
but the sky’s just painted rust.
we’re the gears in the grind,
but the hands don’t know our names.
just another ghost
in the ledger of the game.

*(verse 2)*
the news is loud and bright,
but the truth’s a flicker.
they count the votes at night,
but the people stay a whisper.
we march, we scream, we bleed,
but the system’s got no ears.
just a smile, just a check,
just another year.

*(pre-chorus)*
oh, they say be patient,
like the wounds just heal with time.
but the clock’s a thief, love,
and the rich don’t do the crime.

*(chorus)*
this is bread and circus,
feed the machine, keep us hungry.
promise us the moon,
but the sky’s just painted rust.
we’re the gears in the grind,
but the hands don’t know our names.
just another ghost
in the ledger of the game.

*(bridge)*
so we dance in the ashes,
of the dreams they sold us cheap.
a little bit of hope,
just enough to keep us weak.
but the fire’s in the wires,
and the spark’s in the street.
one day the lights go out,
and the whole damn thing repeats.

*(outro)*
this is bread and circus…
this is bread and circus…
this is bread and circus…
until the circus burns.
